mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate compresses consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising unemployment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.50 percent, markets price in tighter financial conditions that increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 0.50 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical episodes where both series show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in industrial production directly reduces supply chain bottlenecks and input cost inflation, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observed episodes, breaking the supply-side transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.8 percentage points signals rapid labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.8 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: Monthly unemployment drops exceeding 0.8 percentage points are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates combined with rising residential fixed investment indicate accommodative financial conditions fueling real estate and construction demand, which lifts related equity sectors and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined low-rate and high-investment signal predicts negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of out-of-sample periods, indicating a broken monetary-transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are consistently followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, invalidating the demand-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion phases, concurrent rises in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirm synchronized supply and labor recovery, which compresses risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising production and employment fails to produce positive 20-day equity returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes, indicating decoupled macro-financial linkages.
